Navigation: Swag Labs is an e-commerce testing platform with a clear navigational structure. The site uses a simple single-page-application layout. Users begin at the sign-in page, which displays the Swag Labs logo at the top, followed by Username and Password input fields and a Login button. Upon successful login, users are taken to the product inventory page, which has a persistent header bar containing the hamburger menu icon (three horizontal lines) on the far left, the "Swag Labs" title in the center, and a shopping cart icon on the far right showing a badge count of items added. The hamburger menu slides open from the left to reveal four navigation links: "All Items" which returns to the product inventory, "About" which links to the Sauce Labs website, "Logout" which ends the session and returns to the login page, and "Reset App State" which clears the cart and resets filters. A close button (X) at the top right of the menu dismisses it. From the inventory page, users can click any product name or image to navigate to its product detail page, which includes a "Back to products" button to return to the inventory. Users can add items to the cart from both the inventory and detail pages using "Add to cart" buttons, which change to "Remove" buttons once an item is added. Clicking the shopping cart icon navigates to the cart page, where users can continue shopping or proceed to checkout. The checkout is a multi-step process: Checkout Step One collects personal information, Checkout Step Two shows the order summary, and the final confirmation page completes the purchase.

Functional Overview.1. Login: The login page displays the Swag Labs logo prominently at the top center of the page, followed by a green "Swag Labs" heading. Below the heading, a login form contains a Username text input field with placeholder text "Username", a Password text input field (masked) with placeholder text "Password", and a green "Login" button spanning the full width of the form. Below the form, a section displays accepted usernames (standard_user, locked_out_user, problem_user, performance_glitch_user, error_user, visual_user) and the shared password (secret_sauce) for testing purposes. When the user enters valid credentials and clicks Login, the system authenticates against the stored user database and redirects to the product inventory page. If the credentials are invalid, an error message appears in a red banner below the password field with an "X" close button, displaying "Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service." If the username field is empty, the error reads "Epic sadface: Username is required." If only the password field is empty, the error reads "Epic sadface: Password is required." Both input fields receive a red bottom border and a red circular error icon when validation fails. The locked_out_user receives the specific error "Epic sadface: Sorry, this user has been locked out." even with correct credentials.
Functional Overview.2. Product Inventory: The product inventory page serves as the main shopping interface after login. The page header contains the hamburger menu icon on the left, "Swag Labs" title centered, and the shopping cart icon on the right showing a badge with the number of items in the cart (badge is hidden when cart is empty). Below the header, a secondary bar displays the heading "Products" on the left and a sort/filter dropdown on the right defaulting to "Name (A to Z)" with options for "Name (A to Z)", "Name (Z to A)", "Price (low to high)", and "Price (high to low)". The main content area displays a grid of product cards, each containing a product image on the left (clickable, navigates to product detail), the product name as a clickable link in bold text (also navigates to product detail), a description text below the name, the price displayed with a "$" prefix (e.g., "$29.99"), and an "Add to cart" button in the bottom right of each card. When the user clicks "Add to cart", the button changes to a red "Remove" button, the shopping cart icon badge increments by one, and the item is added to the cart. Clicking "Remove" reverses this action. Products available include Sauce Labs Backpack, Sauce Labs Bike Light, Sauce Labs Bolt T-Shirt, Sauce Labs Fleece Jacket, Sauce Labs Onesie, and Test.allTheThings() T-Shirt (Red). Selecting a different sort option immediately reorders the product cards on the page without a page reload.
Functional Overview.3. Product Detail: The product detail page is accessed by clicking a product name or image from the inventory page. The page header remains consistent with the hamburger menu, "Swag Labs" title, and shopping cart icon. Below the header, a "Back to products" button with a left-pointing arrow appears in the top left, allowing the user to return to the product inventory page. The main content area displays the product image on the left side (larger than the inventory thumbnail), and on the right side shows the product name in bold large text as a heading, the full product description text below the name, the price displayed prominently with "$" prefix, and an "Add to cart" button (or "Remove" button if the item is already in the cart). Clicking "Add to cart" adds the item to the shopping cart, updates the cart badge count in the header, and changes the button to "Remove". Clicking "Remove" removes the item from the cart, decrements the badge count, and changes the button back to "Add to cart". The shopping cart icon in the header remains clickable to navigate to the cart page.
Functional Overview.4. Shopping Cart: The shopping cart page is accessed by clicking the shopping cart icon in the top-right corner of the header from any page. The page header remains consistent with the hamburger menu, "Swag Labs" title, and shopping cart icon. Below the header, a secondary bar displays the heading "Your Cart". The cart content area displays a header row with column labels: "QTY" (quantity) and "Description". Each item in the cart is displayed as a row showing the quantity (always "1" as the system does not support multiple quantities), the product name as a clickable link (navigating to the product detail page), the product description text, the price, and a "Remove" button on the right side of each item row. Clicking "Remove" removes the item from the cart, updates the cart badge count, and removes the row from the display. At the bottom of the cart, two buttons appear: a "Continue Shopping" button on the left which navigates back to the product inventory page, and a green "Checkout" button on the right which proceeds to the first step of the checkout process. If the cart is empty, no item rows are displayed but the Continue Shopping and Checkout buttons remain visible.
Functional Overview.5. Checkout: The checkout process consists of three sequential pages. Checkout Step One is accessed by clicking the "Checkout" button from the shopping cart page. The page displays the heading "Checkout: Your Information" below the header bar. The form contains three text input fields: First Name (with placeholder "First Name"), Last Name (with placeholder "Last Name"), and Zip/Postal Code (with placeholder "Zip/Postal Code"). A "Cancel" button on the bottom left returns the user to the shopping cart page without proceeding. A green "Continue" button on the bottom right validates the inputs and proceeds to the next step. If any field is left empty, a red error banner appears below the fields displaying "Error: First Name is required", "Error: Last Name is required", or "Error: Postal Code is required" respectively, with an "X" close button and red borders/icons on the empty fields. Checkout Step Two displays the heading "Checkout: Overview" and shows a summary of all cart items with their quantities, names, descriptions, and prices (same layout as the cart page). Below the items, a "Payment Information" section displays the payment method (e.g., "SauceCard #31337"), a "Shipping Information" section displays the shipping method (e.g., "Free Pony Express Delivery!"), and a "Price Total" section shows the Item total, Tax, and Total amount. A "Cancel" button returns to the inventory page, and a green "Finish" button completes the purchase. Upon clicking Finish, the user is taken to the Checkout Complete page which displays a green checkmark icon, the heading "Thank you for your order!", a descriptive message "Your order has been dispatched, and will arrive just as fast as the pony can get there!", and a "Back Home" button that clears the cart and returns to the product inventory page.
Functional Overview.Logout: The logout function is accessed by first clicking the hamburger menu icon (three horizontal lines) in the top-left corner of the header bar, which slides open a side navigation panel from the left. The panel displays four navigation links vertically: "All Items", "About", "Logout", and "Reset App State", with a close button (X) at the top right of the panel. Clicking the "Logout" link immediately ends the user's authenticated session, clears any cart data or application state, and redirects the user to the login page where the Username and Password fields are empty and the Swag Labs logo is displayed. After logout, any attempt to navigate directly to an authenticated page redirects the user back to the login page with the error message "Epic sadface: You can only access '/inventory.html' when you are logged in." This ensures that no product selections, cart contents, or user data remain accessible after the session ends.
